human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for sportsbook-specific workflow depth, ticket and account evidence quality, payout and reconciliation control discipline, AML and responsible-gaming escalation posture, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to sportsbook ticket, wagering-account, settlement, payout, reconciliation, and player-service exception handling rather than trading, cage approval, AML disposition, or legal interpretation.
Authority sources cover Nevada and New Jersey sportsbook regulation, Nevada race and sports internal controls, FinCEN AML guidance, GLI event-wagering controls, and responsible-gaming standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal sportsbook execution, missing evidence, unsupported rescissions, payout completion without proof, reconciliation mismatches, suspicious activity escalation, responsible-gaming blockers, policy conflicts, adjacent-lane rejection, and tenant-specific channel adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/casinos-gaming-and-wagering/sportsbook-operations-specialist/ so sportsbook operations work is distinct from adjacent trading, compliance, cage, surveillance, responsible-gaming, and general casino lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around unsupported wager rescissions, false completion attempts, suspicious activity, responsible-gaming blockers, and source-conflict escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated sportsbook operations specialist for ticket, account, payout, and reconciliation execution with explicit cash, account, and compliance boundaries.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
